mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135230.0 as of Dec 2011 shows labor market recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, the zero-lower-bound constraint signals monetary accommodation that suppresses risk-free rates and forces capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.99% as of Dec 2011 indicates pre-crisis baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 0.3pp) signals demand destruction that forces equity valuations lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363806.0 and UNRATE at 4.6% as of Dec 2011
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term (RSAFS decline × UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side capacity utilization that improves corporate margins and cash flow, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as profit expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.0991 as of Nov 2011 shows post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.5 million annualized units while simultaneously real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, indicating a demand-constrained economy where equity risk premiums widen, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.5M annualized AND GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1957.0 and GDPC1 at 11403.6 as of Nov 2011
regime_hint: weak demand regime with housing contraction
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below inflation, signaling negative real rates that erode bond returns and redirect capital to equities as inflation hedge, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY growth
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term (inflation > 2.5% AND real rates negative) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates below 50,000 jobs per month, signaling labor market deterioration that compresses equity valuations through lower earnings and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and PAYEMS at 135230.0 as of Dec 2011
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while simultaneously the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises, signaling stagflationary pressure where supply constraints meet demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.3% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp within 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.0991 and UNRATE at 4.6% as of Nov-Dec 2011
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or supply shock regime
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 58% of cases, or the interaction term (INDPRO decline × UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
